# Test -e flag: explain why targets are out of date

# Target does not exist (prereq exists)
exec touch prereq
mk -e target
stderr 'mk: target does not exist'

# Virtual target is explained
mk -e virtual
stderr 'mk: virtual is virtual'

# Up to date target (target newer than prereq)
exec touch prereq
exec touch target
mk -e target
stderr 'mk: target is up to date'

# Older than prereq
exec sleep 0.1
exec touch prereq
mk -e target
stderr 'mk: target older than prereq'

# Stale because prereq was rebuilt (chain: final depends on mid depends on base)
exec touch base
exec touch mid
exec touch final
# Make base newer than mid so mid gets rebuilt, which makes final stale
exec sleep 0.1
exec touch base
mk -e -f chain final
stderr 'mk: mid older than base'
stderr 'mk: final stale because mid was rebuilt'

# P attribute: custom staleness program says out of date
cp pdep pdep
cp ptarget ptarget
mk -e -p 1 -f pmkfile ptarget
stderr 'mk: ptarget out of date via pdep \(P attribute\)'

# Forced by -a flag
exec touch prereq
exec touch target
mk -e -a target
stderr 'mk: target forced by -a/-w flag'

-- mkfile --
target: prereq
	echo building target

prereq:

virtual:V:
	echo virtual recipe

-- pmkfile --
ptarget:Psh -c 'exit 1': pdep
	echo rebuilding ptarget
-- pdep --
content
-- ptarget --
content
-- chain --
final: mid
	echo building final
mid: base
	echo building mid
base:
